scope: DeepSeek-V4 shared latent-KV sparse MLA backward compressed-prefix dKV accumulation

commands.s128_mixed_chunk32: HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python3 benchmark_sparse_mla_shared_kv_compressed_dkv_bmm_floor.py --seq-len 128 --batch 1 --heads 64 --dim 512 --compress-ratio 4 --topk 512 --dtype bf16 --math-dtype fp32 --bmm-input-dtype bf16 --seq-chunk 32 --warmup-iters 1 --timed-iters 3 --json
commands.s4096_mixed_chunk_sweep: HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python3 benchmark_sparse_mla_shared_kv_compressed_dkv_bmm_floor.py --seq-len 4096 --batch 1 --heads 64 --dim 512 --compress-ratio 4 --topk 512 --dtype bf16 --math-dtype fp32 --bmm-input-dtype bf16 --seq-chunk {2048,1024,512,256} --warmup-iters 1 --timed-iters 5 --skip-correctness --skip-reference-timing --json

read: Chunking the materialized BMM floor separates two concerns. The full mixed BMM floor is 7.07 ms but peaks at 5.85 GiB. Streaming 2048-token and 1024-token chunks keeps latency in the same band, 7.69-7.72 ms, while reducing peak allocation to 4.51 and 3.88 GiB and shrinking the active score/probability slab to 0.25 and 0.125 GiB. Smaller chunks bend upward: chunk512 is 8.11 ms and chunk256 is 9.51 ms, which exposes repeated BMM launch/packing overhead rather than the original atomic wall. The production design should preserve MFMA-friendly KxS @ SxD accumulation inside a fused streaming schedule, not call many external BMMs and not materialize the full [B,H,S,K] slab.
